content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach avoids all major AoA anti-patterns. They refuse to validate the trap (no 'yes, you really are stuck'), refuse to strategize about the boss/system (no 'have you tried HR?'), refuse to offer false reassurance, and do not get baited into debating whether the constraints are real (turn 6: 'You're right that I don't know this place' — graceful decline of the bait). No fixing-people, no evangelizing AoA terminology, no view-as-technique (the signature question in turn 2 lands with warmth in turn 1's acknowledgment), no wonder-as-strategy (questions are spaced and connective, not rapid-fire extraction), no fighting-the-voice, no safety-as-control. When the client gets defensive in turn 7 ('so what, it's me?'), the coach does not over-explain or apologize — they acknowledge the twelve years as real and gently return to the laugh. No loving-to-transform escalation when nothing was shifting; the coach stayed quiet and patient through turns 10-12.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Empathic reflection before the signature question — warmth present, not view-as-technique."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines bait to debate constraints ('You're right that I don't know this place') and names the pattern without strategizing."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Honors the twelve years rather than dismissing or arguing — avoids fixing and avoids debate."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Lets the feared possibility sit rather than reassuring it away — avoids false reassurance trap."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Quiet, spacious invitation; no push for breakthrough despite stuckness — avoids loving-to-transform."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal intervention to deepen contact with the feeling already arriving."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "fight-or-roll-over-binary", "trappedness-feeling"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["fear-triangle", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome"],
  "notes": "Positive avoidance visible: client actively baits the coach in turns 5 and 7 to either validate the trap or get into debate, and the coach declines both gracefully."
}
